author: Emma Harper
title: Chicken Bacon Ranch Pasta
ingredients: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts (cut into 1-inch pieces)
instructions_list: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the penne pasta and cook according to package directions until al dente. Drain the pasta and set it aside.

While the pasta cooks, chop the bacon into small pieces. Cook the bacon in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels, leaving the bacon drippings in the skillet.

Add the butter to the skillet with the bacon drippings. Once melted, add the chicken pieces in a single layer. Season with garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink inside.
Sprinkle the flour over the chicken and stir to coat. Cook for 1 minute to remove the raw flour taste. Gradually pour in the milk while stirring constantly to create a smooth sauce.
Stir in the ranch dressing and shredded cheddar cheese. Continue cooking and stirring until the cheese is melted and the sauce has thickened slightly, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked pasta and crispy bacon to the skillet. Gently toss everything together until the pasta is evenly coated in the sauce. Cook for another minute until heated through.
Remove from heat and garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
description: This creamy Chicken Bacon Ranch Pasta is a quick 35-minute meal with tender chicken, crispy bacon, and rich ranch sauce. Perfect for busy weeknights!
